name: b2c-vs-extension
description: Tools for developing on Salesforce B2C Commerce, including cartridge deployment, script debugging and IntelliSense, and sandbox management.

semanticTokenTypes.description: An ISML tag name (e.g. isloop, isprint), highlighted distinctly from HTML tags.

b2c-dx.features.sandboxFilesystem.description: Automatically mount the active instance's WebDAV filesystem as a workspace folder on startup. Requires the WebDAV Browser to be enabled.

b2c-dx.features.jobsExplorer.description: (Preview) Enable the Job History view for monitoring Business Manager job execution history. In development — off by default.

b2c-dx.features.apiBrowser.description: Enable the API Browser for exploring SCAPI schemas.

b2c-dx.features.exportExplorer.description: (Preview) Enable the Export view for interactively exporting site impex data units. In development — off by default.

b2c-dx.features.cap.description: Enable Commerce App Package (CAP) commands and file decorations.

b2c-dx.logLevel.description: Log verbosity level for the B2C DX output channel.

b2c-dx.sandbox.pollingInterval.description: Seconds between polls during sandbox state transitions.

b2c-dx.jobs.refreshInterval.description: Seconds between background refreshes for the Jobs view.

b2c-dx.jobs.discoveryExecutionScanLimit.description: Maximum number of recent executions scanned to discover unique jobs in the Jobs view.

b2c-dx.jobs.defaultStatusFilter.description: Default status filter for Job History root items. 'all' shows every recent execution (the BM-style timeline view).

enumDescriptions: BM-style timeline: every execution is a top-level row, newest first.
Group executions under their parent Job ID, with the most-recently-run job on top.
b2c-dx.jobs.defaultGrouping.description: Default root grouping for Job History. Use the title-bar toggle to switch at runtime.

b2c-dx.jobs.autoRefresh.description: Auto-refresh Job History on a schedule (set by b2c-dx.jobs.refreshInterval). Off by default — load manually from the title bar.

b2c-dx.jobs.knownJobIds.description: Optional list of known job IDs shown as quick picks when running a job from VS Code.

b2c-dx.features.codeSync.description: Enable Code Sync (watch and deploy cartridges).

b2c-dx.features.scriptTypes.description: Provide Script API IntelliSense (dw/*) in cartridge JavaScript via the bundled TypeScript Server plugin. No files are written to your workspace.

b2c-dx.telemetry.enabled.description: Send anonymous usage telemetry (extension lifecycle and broad feature-category events). Honors VS Code's telemetry.telemetryLevel — disabling that disables this regardless of this setting.

b2c-dx.features.cipAnalytics.description: (Preview) Enable the B2C-DX Analytics (CIP) sidebar for browsing tables and running curated reports. In development — off by default.

b2c-dx.features.onboarding.description: (Preview) Enable the guided developer onboarding walkthrough and role-based deep-dive panel. In development — off by default.

b2c-dx.features.xmlValidation.description: Enable XSD-based validation, autocomplete, and hover docs for B2C metadata XML files. Requires the Red Hat XML extension (redhat.vscode-xml), which this extension offers to install when you open a metadata XML file.

b2c-dx.features.ismlFormatting.description: Provide a document formatter for ISML files (Format Document / Format Selection), using the same engine as VS Code's built-in HTML formatter.

b2c-dx.isml.highlightTags.description: Highlight ISML tag names (e.g. <isloop>, <isprint>) distinctly from plain HTML tags. Color follows the active theme's `ismlTag` semantic token color; disable to render ISML tags in the normal HTML tag color.

b2c-dx.isml.format.wrapLineLength.description: Maximum line length before ISML formatting wraps. Set to 0 to disable wrapping.

b2c-dx.isml.format.wrapAttributes.description: How ISML formatting wraps element attributes (mirrors html.format.wrapAttributes).

b2c-dx.isml.format.preserveNewLines.description: Preserve existing blank lines when formatting ISML.

b2c-dx.isml.format.indentInnerHtml.description: Indent the content of <head> and <body> sections when formatting ISML (mirrors html.format.indentInnerHtml).

b2c-dx.isml.diagnostics.disabledRules.markdownDescription: ISML diagnostic rule codes to disable globally. Empty by default (all rules on, including the `encoding-off` output-escaping security warning). Add a code to silence that rule; individual lines can also be suppressed inline with `<iscomment> b2c-dx-disable-next-line <code> </iscomment>`.

viewsWelcome.contents:
No B2C Commerce instance configured.

[Set up your instance](command:workbench.action.openWalkthrough?%5B%22Salesforce.b2c-vs-extension%23b2c-dx.gettingStarted%22%2C%22configure-instance%22%5D)

[Refresh](command:b2c-dx.webdav.refresh)

No content libraries configured.

[Set up your instance](command:workbench.action.openWalkthrough?%5B%22Salesforce.b2c-vs-extension%23b2c-dx.gettingStarted%22%2C%22configure-instance%22%5D) to enable content browsing, then add a library.

[Add Library](command:b2c-dx.content.addLibrary)

Browse SCAPI OpenAPI schemas for your Commerce Cloud instance.

Requires OAuth credentials (`client-id`, `client-secret`, `shortCode`) in dw.json.

[Connect & authenticate](command:workbench.action.openWalkthrough?%5B%22Salesforce.b2c-vs-extension%23b2c-dx.gettingStarted%22%2C%22connect%22%5D)

[Load APIs](command:b2c-dx.apiBrowser.refresh)

No sandbox realms configured.

Set "realm" in dw.json or add a realm manually.

[Add Realm](command:b2c-dx.sandbox.addRealm)

Sandbox management requires Account Manager OAuth credentials.

Add "client-id" and "client-secret" to dw.json to browse and manage sandboxes for this realm.

[Connect & authenticate](command:workbench.action.openWalkthrough?%5B%22Salesforce.b2c-vs-extension%23b2c-dx.gettingStarted%22%2C%22connect%22%5D)

Job monitoring requires OAuth credentials with OCAPI job scopes.

Add "client-id" and "client-secret" to dw.json, then ensure your API client has access to `/job_execution_search` and `/jobs/*/executions*`.

[Connect & authenticate](command:workbench.action.openWalkthrough?%5B%22Salesforce.b2c-vs-extension%23b2c-dx.gettingStarted%22%2C%22connect%22%5D)

Jobs view is not loaded by default to avoid unwanted OCAPI traffic.

[Refresh Now](command:b2c-dx.jobs.refresh)
[Enable Auto-Refresh](command:b2c-dx.jobs.toggleAutoRefresh)
[Run Job](command:b2c-dx.jobs.run)
[Open in Business Manager](command:b2c-dx.jobs.openBmDefinitions)

No cartridges found.

Cartridges are identified by .project files in the workspace.

[Generate from a scaffold](command:workbench.action.openWalkthrough?%5B%22Salesforce.b2c-vs-extension%23b2c-dx.gettingStarted%22%2C%22scaffold%22%5D)

[Refresh](command:b2c-dx.codeSync.refreshCartridges)

Explore Commerce Intelligence Platform (CIP) data — build SQL queries visually, browse tables, and run curated reports.

[Open Query Builder](command:b2c-dx.cipAnalytics.queryBuilder)
[Browse Tables](command:b2c-dx.cipAnalytics.browseTables)

Requires OAuth credentials (clientId, clientSecret) in dw.json.

[Refresh](command:b2c-dx.cipAnalytics.refresh)

Interactively export site impex data units.

Requires OAuth and WebDAV credentials in dw.json. Check the data units you want, then run Export.

[Refresh](command:b2c-dx.export.refresh)

cartridgePath.description: Path to cartridges directory (auto-detected from workspace by default)

commands.title: Open Job Definitions in Business Manager
Reset B2C Commerce Root to Auto-Detect
Reset Setup Session (Start over from connection step)

walkthroughs.title: B2C DX — Developer Onboarding
walkthroughs.description: A guided 5-phase setup — install, configure, connect, deploy, scaffold — for the Salesforce B2C Commerce Developer Experience extension. Most teams complete this in under 30 minutes.

steps.title: Phase 0 · Choose your role
steps.description:
Different roles need different things first. Open the **role-based deep-dive** for a path tailored to how you'll use B2C Commerce — Storefront, API/Integration, DevOps/Release, or AI-augmented developer. Or skip ahead and follow the universal five-phase flow below.

[Open role-based guide](command:b2c-dx.onboarding.open)

Already set up? [Mark all phases as done](command:b2c-dx.walkthrough.markAllDone).

media.altText: B2C DX wordmark above a vertical five-step timeline: Install the B2C CLI (optional), Configure your instance (dw.json), Connect and authenticate, Deploy your first cartridge, and Generate from a scaffold.

steps.title: Phase 1 · Install the B2C CLI
steps.description:
**Optional.** The B2C CLI powers deploys, log tailing, and sandbox commands from the terminal. Skip if you'll only use the VS Code views.

Install via `npm install -g @salesforce/b2c-cli` or `brew install salesforcecommercecloud/tools/b2c-cli`. 

Requires Node.js 22+.

[Verify installation](command:b2c-dx.cli.verify) · [Update to latest](command:b2c-dx.cli.update)

steps.title: Phase 2 · Configure your instance
steps.description:
Run the configuration wizard. Non-secret fields (hostname, code-version, short-code, tenant-id, mrtProject, mrtEnvironment) go into `dw.json`; secret pairs (OAuth `client-id`+`client-secret`, Basic `username`+`password`, MRT API key) are placed independently — Keychain, `pass`, env vars, or dw.json — per the documented Credential Grouping rule.

[Run setup wizard](command:b2c-dx.walkthrough.chooseCredentialStorage) · [Inspect resolved config](command:b2c-dx.walkthrough.inspectSetup) · [Create dw.json (manual)](command:b2c-dx.walkthrough.createDwJson)

steps.title: Phase 3 · Connect & authenticate
steps.description:
Verify your config resolves and the extension can reach your instance. The active instance shows in the status bar (bottom-left).

OAuth fields (`client-id`, `client-secret`, `short-code`) unlock the Sandbox Explorer and API Browser; basic auth (`username`, `password`) is enough for WebDAV and cartridge deploys.

[Inspect resolved config](command:b2c-dx.walkthrough.inspectSetup) — runs `b2c setup inspect` and shows where each value came from (file / env / keychain).

[Inspect active instance](command:b2c-dx.instance.inspect) · [Open Realm Explorer](command:workbench.view.extension.b2c-dx-sandboxes)

steps.title: Phase 4 · Deploy your first cartridge
steps.description:
Cartridges are folders containing a `.project` file. The extension auto-detects them in your workspace and lists them under the Cartridges view.

[Deploy all cartridges](command:b2c-dx.codeSync.deploy)

[Open Cartridges view](command:workbench.view.extension.b2c-dx)

steps.title: Phase 5 · Generate from a scaffold
steps.description:
Generate boilerplate for cartridges, controllers, models, and Page Designer pages without leaving VS Code.

Right-click a folder in the Explorer → **B2C DX → New from Scaffold…** or run the command directly.

[New from Scaffold…](command:b2c-dx.scaffold.generate)

---

Finished early? [Mark all steps as done](command:b2c-dx.walkthrough.markAllDone).

scripts.vscode:prepublish: pnpm run typecheck:agent && pnpm --filter @salesforce/b2c-script-types run build && pnpm --filter @salesforce/b2c-tooling-sdk run build && node scripts/esbuild-bundle.mjs
scripts.package: pnpm run build && pnpm exec vsce package --no-dependencies && node scripts/inject-script-types.mjs
